findings.root_cause: Return value (uint8_t) from training flows into 7-bit register field mask without validation
findings.symbolic_insight: Symbolic hook at boundary produces unconstrained 32-bit value; mask truncates >127 silently

findings.root_cause: Same pattern: 8-bit return value masked to 7 bits without clamping
findings.symbolic_insight: Boundary hook reveals unconstrained value propagation to sensitive register write

findings.root_cause: Caller has no validation between training result and register write
findings.evidence: Lines 170-184 of ddr5_init_controller.c — no bounds check on training results
findings.symbolic_insight: Entry point allows unconstrained symbolic values to reach hardware register writes

findings.root_cause: FIRST write to MR_DATA happens before MR_CMD barrier; tMRD waits AFTER final register write
findings.evidence: Line 296-308 of ddr5_init_controller.c: MR_DATA is set before MR_CMD but timing only applies after
findings.symbolic_insight: Write ordering could be exploited if symbolic values trigger race condition in controller

summary.stage5_unreproducible_reason: These findings require spec-model knowledge (state definitions, recovery expectations) that cannot be inferred from symbolic execution alone. They are inherently static analysis concerns.
